title: pothos scandens var. cognatus
intro: Pothos scandens var. cognatus, also known as Manjari or Manjari vine, is a type of flowering plant native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Pothos scandens var. cognatus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
